name: Ventilation mode - High-frequency
description: High-frequency ventilation modes (oscillatory HFOV, jet HFJV, positive-pressure HFPPV).

en.name: Ventilation mode - High-frequency
en.shortDescription: High-frequency ventilation modes (oscillatory HFOV, jet HFJV, positive-pressure HFPPV).
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

High-frequency ventilation modes, which deliver very small tidal volumes at rates well above normal breathing — high-frequency oscillatory (HFOV), high-frequency jet (HFJV) and high-frequency positive-pressure ventilation (HFPPV). In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating the ventilation mode in use; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. The mode is independent of whether ventilation is invasive (via an endotracheal/tracheostomy tube) or non-invasive (via a mask) — that distinction is captured in the Invasive mechanical ventilation and Non-invasive ventilation concept sets.

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED procedure concepts for these modes.

- *4055376 / 243153009 — High frequency positive pressure ventilation (SNOMED)*
- *4055377 / 243154003 — High frequency jet ventilation (SNOMED)*
- *4074666 / 243155002 — High frequency oscillatory ventilation (SNOMED)*

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded ventilation mode to the matching concept here based on the control variable / mode family, regardless of whether the patient is invasively or non-invasively ventilated.
- **Mode vs ventilation act**: this set captures the *mode* only; whether the patient is on invasive or non-invasive ventilation is recorded separately (Invasive mechanical ventilation / Non-invasive ventilation concept sets).
